name: search
description:
Search for files in the user's Google Drive using the Google Drive API (files.list with query filters). Returns metadata for matching files including file_id, name, mime_type, web_view_link, size_bytes, modified_time, owner_name, and a readable flag.

When to use: the user mentions their files, documents, spreadsheets, slides, PDFs, notes, or reports; asks to find, search, list, or summarize their documents; references 'my files' or 'my docs'; asks for recent or starred files; or anything that could be in Google Drive.

Search behavior:
- By default, the query performs full-text search on Google Workspace files (Docs, Sheets, Slides) and title-only matching on non-Google files (PDFs, Word, images, etc.).
- Folders are excluded from results by default. To find folders, you must set mime_type_filter to 'application/vnd.google-apps.folder'.
- Trashed files are always excluded.
- When a text query is provided, results are ranked by relevance. When no query is provided (filter-only search), results are sorted by most recently modified first.
- exact_name takes priority over query. If both are provided, only exact_name is used for name matching.

Reading file content: When the user wants to read, summarize, or analyze file content (not just find files), set include_content to true. This inlines the text of the top readable results (Google Docs as Markdown, Sheets as CSV, Slides as text, plus text/JSON/XML files). For files not readable inline (PDFs, Office docs, images), use google_drive_read_file with the file_id from the search results.

Common patterns:
- Find files by keyword: provide query (e.g. "quarterly report")
- Find file by exact name: use exact_name (e.g. "Budget 2026.xlsx")
- List recent files: omit query, optionally set modified_after
- List starred files: omit query, set starred to true
- Find folders: set mime_type_filter to 'application/vnd.google-apps.folder' with query or exact_name
- Search within a folder: set folder_name (e.g. "Projects")
- Files user personally edited: set modified_by_me to true

Do NOT set owner_email or shared_with_me unless the user explicitly asks about file ownership or sharing. General queries like 'my files' should search all accessible files.

query.description: Free-text search query. Matches file titles for all file types; also matches body content for Google Docs, Sheets, and Slides. Omit when using filter-only searches (starred, modified_after, shared_with_me, etc.). Ignored if exact_name is set.

max_results.description: Maximum number of files to return (default 10, max 50)

mime_type_filter.description: Filter results to a specific MIME type. Required to find folders (use 'application/vnd.google-apps.folder') since folders are excluded by default. Common values: 'application/vnd.google-apps.document' (Google Docs), 'application/vnd.google-apps.spreadsheet' (Google Sheets), 'application/vnd.google-apps.presentation' (Google Slides), 'application/pdf' (PDFs), 'application/vnd.google-apps.folder' (folders).

modified_after.description: Only return files modified after this ISO 8601 date/time (e.g. '2026-03-01T00:00:00Z'). When used without a query, results are sorted by most recently modified first.

modified_before.description: Only return files modified before this ISO 8601 date/time (e.g. '2026-03-19T23:59:59Z')

folder_id.description: Restrict search to files within this Google Drive folder ID

folder_name.description: Search within a specific folder by its name (e.g. 'Projects', 'NASDAQ'). Use this when the user mentions a folder name. Automatically resolves to the folder ID. For nested folders, use the most specific folder name.

page_token.description: Pagination token from a previous search response's next_page_token

include_content.description: When true, reads and inlines text content of the top 3 readable results (up to 10000 chars each). Readable formats: Google Docs (as Markdown), Google Sheets (as CSV), Google Slides (as text), plus text/*, JSON, XML, YAML files. For PDFs or Office docs, use google_drive_read_file separately. Set this when the user wants to read, summarize, or analyze content — not just find files.

modified_by_me.description: When true, sort by files the user personally edited (not files edited by collaborators). Use for queries like 'files I edited', 'my recent edits', 'documents I changed'.

owner_email.description: Filter to files owned by a specific email. Use 'me' for files the user created/owns. Do NOT set this for general queries like 'my files' — those include shared files too. Only use when the user explicitly asks about file ownership.

shared_with_me.description: When true, ONLY return files shared with the user (excludes files they own). Do NOT set for general queries — leave false to search all accessible files.

starred.description: When true, only return starred/favorited files. Can be used alone (without query) to list all starred files.

title_only.description: When true, search only file titles/names instead of full-text content search. Faster and more precise for finding files by name.

exact_name.description: Match a file by its exact name (e.g. 'Q1 Budget.xlsx'). If set, the query parameter is ignored. Use when the user provides a specific filename.

created_after.description: Only return files created after this ISO 8601 date/time. Use for 'files created this week' queries. Different from modified_after which tracks edits.

created_before.description: Only return files created before this ISO 8601 date/time.

visibility.description: Filter by sharing visibility. Values: 'anyoneWithLink', 'anyoneCanFind', 'domainWithLink', 'domainCanFind', 'limited'. Use for 'publicly shared files' queries.

writer_email.description: Filter to files where a specific email has write/edit access. Example: 'jane@company.com'
